Functionality: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers AI-driven autocompletion, which uses historical data and machine learning to provide intelligent recommendations for completing sales order data. 

Example: The system can suggest the correct shipping address, payment terms, or material details based on past orders with similar characteristics. 

SAP Fiori App: The "Monitor Recommendations for Sales Document Completion" app provides a central entry point to view incomplete sales orders and corresponding data field recommendations. 

4. Importing Sales Orders from Excel Spreadsheets:

Functionality: You can use the SAP Fiori app "Import Sales Orders" to batch-create sales orders directly from Excel files. 

Process:

    • Upload the Excel file containing the sales order data. 
    • The system automatically creates sales order requests and populates the data. 
    • You can then simulate or create the sales order. 

SAP BTP Use Case:  SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) can be used to automate sales order creation from Excel files, leveraging robotic process automation (RPA) to extract data from Excel and create sales order documents in SAP S/4HANA. 

5. Automating Sales Order Creation from Unstructured Data:

Functionality: You can use SAP Build Process Automation to create an RPA bot to automate the processing of sales order requests received in the form of unstructured files like PDFs, images, etc

 

 Process:

    • The bot scans emails for keywords in the subject line. 
    • It extracts attachments from the emails and uploads them to the "Create Sales Orders - Automatic Extraction" app. 
    • The app creates sales order requests and uploads the email attachments to a document information extraction service. 
    • The service extracts relevant information from the email attachments. 

6. SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, duplicate detection

Sales agents can leverage the feature that performs a check, comparing the configured fields with the list of existing accounts within the system. As an administrator, they can configure the duplicate check for accounts. Once configured, any new account that is created is checked against the configured fields and the result displays a list of the existing accounts with a confidence score, which enables sales agents to take a decision whether or not to proceed with the account creation.

Benefits

    • Prevent from creating more duplicate records, improving data accuracy
    • Ensure a cleaner database for operations and sales forecasts

entry.title: Updates to T4A-NR Withholding Tax Report (Canada)
entry.content:
Introduction

Each year, the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) updates the T4A-NR form, summary, and associated electronic filing requirements to reflect evolving tax laws, improve clarity, and address user feedback. For the 2025 tax filing season, CRA has implemented several important changes, impacting how businesses report payments to non-residents for services rendered in Canada.

Understanding the T4A-NR Slip: A Refresher

Before we dive into the changes, let's quickly recap the importance of the T4A-NR slip. This critical tax form is used to report amounts paid to non-residents for services rendered in Canada, helping ensure accurate income reporting, compliance, and reliable tax filing for non-residents.

Both the T4A-NR slip and the T4A-NR-SUM form, which summarizes the total amounts reported on all T4A-NR slips for each recipient, are subject to updates, including changes to their layout, instructions, and required information.

Updates to the T4A-NR, T4A-NR-SUM Forms:

While the T4A-NR form (24) saw no changes to the tax boxes themselves, there were updates to the completion guidelines for:

  • T4A-NR Withholding Tax Slip (FIWTCA_T4ANR_INT_SLIP)  
  • T4A-NR Withholding Tax summary Slip (FIWTCA_T4ANR_SUMMARY_SLIP)
  • T4A-NR Withholding Tax Slip (FIWTCA_T4ANR_SLIP)

Additionally, electronic filing became mandatory for businesses submitting more than 5 information returns, a significant reduction from the previous threshold of 50. The changes ensure an improvement to the accuracy and efficiency of reporting payments made to non-residents for services performed in Canada. 

Key Updates Electronic Transmittal XML Filing:

The T619 XML file, used for electronically transmitting various tax return types (including the T4A-NR), now requires adherence to significantly revised XML filing requirements, designed to enhance data security and integrity:

  • XML Schema Updates:  The CRA has implemented stricter XML schema validation rules. This requires comprehensive data field population and validation before submission, significantly reducing the risk of rejected filings.
  • Field Changes: Pay close attention to the following specific field changes:
    • Fields Removed: Key identifiers such as Report Type Code and Transmitter number have been eliminated.
    • Fields Updated: Transmitter name and Transmitter Country Code adjustments for precision.
    • New Fields: Newly introduced fields include TransmitterAccountNumber and TransmitterRepID, demanding attention to configuration.

Note: all returns filed in the same submission must be the same information return type. For example, a T4A return cannot be submitted together with a T4 return in the same electronic file.

 Why These Changes Matter

Failing to use the correct T4A-NR form or adhering to the updated XML filing requirements can result in:

  • Penalties: The CRA can impose penalties for non-compliance with tax regulations.
  • Processing Delays: Incorrect or incomplete filings can lead to delays in processing tax returns, both for your business and the non-residents you are paying.
  • Audit Risks: Non-compliance can increase the likelihood of a CRA audit.

How SAP Helps Businesses Stay Compliant 

SAP's Globalization team supports customers using SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP DRC to address regulatory changes by:

  1. Software Updates: SAP delivers regular updates and support packages to ensure that your SAP software reflects the latest legal changes, including updates to XML formats and form layouts.
  2. Documentation: Refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation and SAP Note 3559241 for details of the recent changes to the T4A-NR Report.
  3. Expert Support: SAP offers multiple support channels, including expert chat, community forums, and scheduled sessions with SAP experts, to provide timely assistance with any questions or challenges.
  4. Localization Services: SAP's Globalization Services team ensures that SAP solutions are compliant with Canadian laws and regulatory standards. Leverage the Regulatory Change Manager for agility in adapting to new legal requirements. Furthermore, the SAP Road Map Explorer offers insights into forthcoming updates and feature enhancements.

 Conclusion

The CRA's regular updates to tax and reporting requirements can be challenging for businesses. SAP's proactive and responsive approach helps customers manage these changes effectively. By utilizing SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), businesses can maintain compliance, minimize errors, and optimize their tax reporting processes.

Review the CRA's official T4A-NR guidelines and stay informed through the SAP Localization community page for ongoing updates, valuable resources, and expert discussions.

entry.title: Demystifying SAP ATTP Upgrade, Conversion, Database Migration, and Cloud Transition
entry.content:
Demystifying SAP ATTP Conversion: From NetWeaver to S/4HANA Foundation

SAP has announced that maintenance for SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) based on SAP NetWeaver (software component STTP 300) will end on December 31st, 2027. Although this deadline may seem distant, transitioning to SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals on SAP S/4HANA technology stack offers a strategic opportunity to modernize your serialization landscape, leverage new capabilities, and ensure compliance continuity.

To support this journey, SAP has published a comprehensive conversion guide with step-by-step instructions for migrating to SAP ATTP on S/4HANA.

Why Transition Now?

Moving to SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals on SAP S/4HANA technology stack isn’t just about maintenance timelines, it’s about transforming your serialization operations. SAP is committed to helping companies navigate this shift through close collaboration with our partner ecosystem, combining our platform innovation with specialized implementation expertise to:

  • Simplify your transition options
  • Mitigate risks in regulated environments
  • Accelerate time-to-value with proven best practices methodologies

Clarifying Your Transition Path

Many customers ask: Is this an upgrade, a migration, or a cloud transformation? The answer depends on your current landscape and goals. Below, we break down the four key adoption paths for SAP ATTP on S/4HANA:

The Key Adoption Paths for SAP ATTP on S4HANA Technology Stack 

  1. Upgrading SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) to latest version
  2. Migrating to SAP HANA
  3. Converting SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) to SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals on SAP S/4HANA technology stack
  4. Transitioning to SAP Private Cloud Edition (PCE)

Understanding these options is critical to selecting the right approach for compliance, scalability, and innovation. Let’s explore each in detail.

1. Upgrading SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals ATTP to latest version

The latest versions are:

  • NetWeaver Edition: SAP ATTP 3.0 Feature Pack 04 (recommended version)
  • S/4HANA Foundation Edition: SAP ATTP 2310 FP01 (released July 2024)

The latest version introduces several functional enhancements and technical improvements, including the ability to integrate purchase order numbers from SAP ECC or S/4HANA into SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals Pharmaceuticals (ATTP), enabling accurate reporting of the customer’s purchase order number in the Supply Chain notifications sent to downstream trading partners.

2. Migrating to SAP HANA

Migrating your database to SAP HANA is a prerequisite for converting to SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals on SAP S/4HANA technology stack . You have two options:

  • Migrate the database independently, or
  • Combine the database migration and system conversion into one step

In either case you can leverage a downtime optimized approach to reduce downtime.

This unlocks the performance, real-time analytics, and reduced data footprint benefits of the SAP HANA in-memory platform.

3. Converting SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) to SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals on SAP S/4HANA technology stack

This is a technical conversion of your current system, not a migration. It is only possible if your SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) version is 3.0 or above. If you are on an older version, you must upgrade before proceeding with the conversion.

Here are the key points to understand:

  • Core business data remains untouched. Only the technical stack is converted.
  • The conversion can be done regardless of whether your ERP is ECC or S/4HANA.
  • Tables in the STTP namespace are not impacted by the conversion.
  • If you're already on SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) 3.0 or higher, you can perform the conversion and upgrade in a single step. Otherwise, you’ll need to upgrade first, then convert.

 4. Move to Cloud: SAP Private Cloud Edition

The SAP Private Cloud Edition (PCE) is the dedicated deployment model for SAP SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals ATTP on S/4HANA technology stack and can be implemented independently of conversion. Among its many benefits, the SAP Private Cloud Edition (PCE) offers a comprehensive cloud experience with flexible upgrade options and it simplifies operations by providing software, support, services, and infrastructure, all from a single provider under one unified set of service level agreements (SLAs).

Call to Action SAP ATTP to PCE Converstion

 

Call to Action

Waiting until 2027 to start this journey is risky. Early adopters mitigate the end-of-maintenance crunch and benefit from:

  • Real-time analytics and AI-readiness
  • Optimized performance and user experience
  • Improved compliance and data visibility
  • A clear, controlled roadmap for change with fewer surprises

Final Takeaway

Start your transition early. The sooner you begin planning your upgrade, conversion, and potential move to the cloud, the smoother your transition will be and the sooner you'll unlock the advanced capabilities of SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) on S/4HANA technology stack.  Whether it’s unlocking new analytics capabilities, reducing operational risks, and ensuring long-term compliance.

By working closely with SAP and a certified SAP partner specializing in serialization and value-added solutions, you gain a streamlined transition backed by deep expertise. Together, we help accelerate your timeline, minimize disruption, and mitigate risks through proven methodologies tailored to your business needs.

Don’t wait for the 2027 deadline; take a proactive approach to assess your roadmap, phase implementation costs, and ensure compliance continuity.

entry.title: Incorporating GenAI into SAP Master Data Governance - Central Governance
entry.content:
Overview

The idea of AI capabilities integrated with SAP Master Data Governance is exciting with AI assisted features already delivered by SAP. The possibilities are endless and it is beautifully explained in the blog post by @Mark63 Thoughts About Generative AI in Master Data Governance. One of my key takeaway from the blog post is "the journey of AI integration in SAP Master Data Governance processes has only just begun." 
Simply put, we are in this journey as pioneers & explorers to improve global business processes; one solution at a time.

This got me into thinking and developing a simple use case of "Comprehensive summary of Master Data with key insights using GenAI" for central governance. The driving factor behind the idea was a simple "What if?" Let's dive a little deeper now.

Driving Factor

"What if there existed a unified platform to obtain a thorough overview of master data, featuring essential insights on transactional activities and data quality?" The motivation is to allow users to have an high level overview of master data with a single click of a button.

Solution

AI Summarization of the Master Data elements with a simple button on the SAP MDG Central Governance search screen. 

Considerations

Data Protection: The solution was developed in a sandbox system utilizing dummy data.
LLM's data protection and guidelines should be considered in building solutions around GenAI that includes Data Minimization, Data Isolation and access control.

Conclusion

By leveraging Generative AI, we can create a unified platform that provides users with a comprehensive overview of master data, complete with critical insights into transactional activities and data quality, all at the click of a button. This innovative approach not only streamlines governance processes but also empowers decision-makers with the information they need to drive business success.

As we continue to explore and pioneer this journey, it is essential to remain vigilant about data protection and adhere to best practices in data management. By prioritizing data minimization, isolation, and access control, we can ensure that our solutions are not only effective but also secure. The future of AI in SAP Master Data Governance is bright, and with each step we take, we are laying the groundwork for a more efficient and insightful governance landscape.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Simplifying My Home Activation from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 or higher
entry.content:
Innovation made easier!  Enabling My Home for SAP S/4HANA has been streamlined from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Save time and effort by using the settings in task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4.

In our previous blog, "SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Empowering Your Homepage: Enabling My Home for SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01", we explored the detailed steps required to enable My Home in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. We walked through the structure, technical components, configuration steps, and necessary authorizations to activate My Home manually.

IMPORTANT : If you are looking to understand the structure of My Home i.e. its main components”, or its building blocks and technical components, please refer to our previous blog.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release, the process of enabling My Home has been further streamlined. As a part of this blog, we will focus on the enhancements introduced in FPS02, where Steps 1 through Step 4 described in the previous blog are now automated via the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task list. This significantly reduces manual effort, making My Home activation quicker and more efficient.

Comparison of SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 Task List Across SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, and FPS02

 

Simplified My Home Activation with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02

 

Automation via the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 Task List

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release onwards, Steps 1 through 4 explained in the pervious blog are now automated as part of the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task list. This task list consolidates these key setup steps into a single run, simplifying and streamlining the activation process.

Note:If you have already activated SAP Fiori foundation on your SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 using Rapid Activation and executed the SAP Fiori - Initial Setup for Fiori Application S/4 (SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4) task list, the following tasks are automatically included as part of the default task list run, and no additional manual execution is required.

However, if you have upgraded from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (or lower releases of SAP S/4HANA) to SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, you must re-execute the following four tasks (from the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task list ) described in this blog to ensure My Home is fully enabled with the latest updates.

 

Step (described in the previous blog)Corresponding task from the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task listAutomation Details
1 - Enabling Spaces and Pages LayoutActivate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)Configures FLP property SPACES with system setting checks.
2 - Activating the My Home UI5 ComponentActivate HTTP Services FoundationActivates the SAPUI5 Component ux.eng.s4producthomes1 (SICF node: BSP PRODUCT_HOMES1).
3 - Activating the Required OData V2 and V4 ServicesActivate Gateway OData Services Foundation (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE) & Publish Service Groups Foundation (/IWFND/V4_ADMIN)Publishes the following service groups:
- /IWNGW/NOTIFICATION (Notification)
- /UI2/INSIGHTS_SRV (Insight Cards)
Note: TASKPROCESSING (My Inbox) and C_SITNMYSITUATION_CDS (Situation Handling) are NOT included in this automation and must be activated manually if required.
Please refer to Step 4 from the previous blog for manual activation steps.
4 - Configuring Launchpad ParametersActivate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)Configures all necessary FLP parameters for My Home page, including enablement of the page, Custom Component, and UI5 Insights.

 

Step 1 through Step 4 can be executed cumulatively on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 (or higher)

  1. Navigate to the transaction STC01 (Task Manager for Technical Configuration).
    STC01 : Task Manager for Technical Configuration
  2. Enter the Task List name as SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 (which is used to activate Fiori foundation) and click on the Generate Task List Run (F8) button.

  3. On the next screen with generated task list, uncheck all the tasks using Skip All Tasks (F7) button and then only Select the task Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF). After selecting the task, You will get an Information popup that the activation step has dependencies on other steps like creating transports, activating services etc., click Continue for all the dependent steps.

    Skip all tasks for SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 and select 'Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)' task

     

    By clicking on the Change Parameters button for this task, you could configure the following Fiori Launchpad Parameters required for My Home enablement, which have been highlighted already as a part of the blog. For now, you could just leave them as-is to default and continue.

    Change Parameters for the task 'Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)'
  4. Additionally, also select the task Publish Service Groups Foundation (/IWFND/V4_ADMIN) which would publish the /IWNGW/NOTIFICATION (Notification) and /UI2/INSIGHTS_SRV (Insight Cards) OData V4 services.

    After selecting this task, only the following 5 task should remain selected.
    • Create / Select Customizing Request (SE09)
    • Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)
    • Activate Gateway OData Services Foundation (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE)
    • Publish Service Groups Foundation (/IWFND/V4_ADMIN)
    • Activate HTTP services Foundation
  5. Click on the Start/Resume Task List Run in the Dialog button to start the task list run.

IMPORTANT: As mentioned above, the task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 available with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 would not activate the optional functionality like My Inbox and Situation Handling related OData services. Please follow the references mentioned in the original blog should you be interested to activate them.

Step 5: Assigning Relevant Authorizations via a PFCG Role

 

The role creation and authorization setup for My Home in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 remains the same as FPS01. You can follow the same steps described in the previous blog to create and configure the required PFCG role.

For a quick summary, here are the key steps:

  1. Create a PFCG security role (e.g., Z_SR_MY_HOME)

    • Navigate to transaction PFCG, enter a role name, and click Create Single Role.
    • Provide a role description and switch to the Menu tab.

  2. Add the Required OData Services

    • TASKPROCESSING (for My Inbox)
    • C_SITNMYSITUATION_CDS (for Situations)
    • INTEROP (for personalization, should already be active)
    • /UI2/INSIGHTS_CARDS_READ_SRV (for Insights Cards)

  3. Assign Target Mappings for My Inbox and My Situations

    • Use SAP Fiori Launchpad Content Manager (UI2/FLPCM_CUST).
    • Create a Launchpad Catalog (Z_BC_MY_HOME).
    • Add Target Mappings for My Inbox (F0862) and My Situations (F4154).

  4. Assign the Role to Users

    • Open the Role Maintenance transaction PFCG, go to the User tab, and assign the role to business users.

Note: Ensure that users have business roles containing spaces and pages, so My Home functions correctly.

Note :  

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

entry.title: Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA - New Release (OP2023 - FPS03) Announcement
entry.content:
Our enterprise management layer template based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition OP2023 has been upgraded to FPS03 and will soon be available for customers to buy from SAP.

The new release does not include any major scope item updates (addition, updates, obsolete), but provides a upgraded template with the latest OSS notes provided by FPS03.

In the context of Localization, the following documents/scope items have been updated:

  • EML_2023_DRC01_BPD_EN_XX_V1.3
  • EML_2023_LOC01_BPD_EN_XX_V1.3
  • EML_2023_LOC07_BPD_EN_NO_V1.3
  • EML_2023_LOC11_BPD_EN_CO_V1.3
  • EML_2023_LOC11_BPD_EN_HR_V1.3
  • EML_2023_LOC11_BPD_EN_IN_V1.3
  • EML_2023_LOC11_BPD_EN_PL_V1.3

entry.title: SAP Principal Propagation without Secrets: How Managed Identity in APIM Simplifies Everything
entry.content:
Dear community,

SAP Principal Propagation (for simplicity often also referred to as SSO) is the gold standard for app integration – especially when it comes to 3rd party apps such as Microsoft Power Platform. Building on top of my prior blog on API Management usage for SAP SSO, I am sharing today how you can fully eliminate the passwords or certificates for the token exchange previously required.

No more password storing or rotation. No certificates to renew. No manual key rollover scripts.

Thank god!

The Microsoft Entra ID authentication handshakes with services such as SAP BTP, SuccessFactors, Azure, or M365 apps like Power Platform allows these apps to become native, dynamic, and self-maintaining.

This shift not only tightens security by reducing the attack surface but also significantly accelerates project delivery, improves compliance with modern Zero Trust principles, and cuts down on administrative overhead. It’s a small architectural change with a big real-world impact — and a logical next step for enterprise-grade app integrations in the SAP ecosystem.

 

A glimpse under the hood

The magic? It’s called Managed Identity for Azure. With a user-assigned managed identity in API Management, the platform takes care of the authentication for you — clean, automatic, and secret-free. Building on top of Workload Identity Federation enables using a managed identity as a credential, just like certificate or password, on Entra ID Applications.

See following updated flow diagram from the prior blog. It highlights with red and yellow arrows where it was possible to “eliminate” credentials.

When browsing Microsoft Learn or additional blogs, look for the technical term “Federated Identity Credential” for additional details.

Solving this challenge on Azure API Management level enables scaling the approach to arbitrary many different consumer solutions.

Find the updated policy on the Azure API Management repos for SAP ECC and S/4HANA here and for SuccessFactors here.

Learn more about this space in general from the blog series by my magnificent colleague Martin Raepple.

 

Create a user-assigned managed identity for your Azure API Management instance

 

See additional details on this Microsoft Learn article.

 

Create a “managed credential”

This step is being referred to by Microsoft Learn as Federated Identity Credential (FIC).

  • Click Add Credential as per below screenshot from the manage secrets pane of your Entra ID app registration which represents your API Management instance.
  • Choose scenario “Managed Identity”,
  • Select the id of your user-assigned-managed identity created before, and
  • Make sure the audience is listed as “api://AzureADTokenExchange”.

 

Import the updated policy

Navigate to your SAP OData API on Azure API Management and use the Azure portal UI to paste the policy as code, or leverage infrastructure-as-code with your devops pipeline to apply SAP Principal Propagation at scale. See this Azure Developer CLI sample using Bicep for reference.

See the APIM policy line doing the magical managed identity request. It is as simple as that:

<authentication-managed-identity resource="api://azureadtokenexchange" client-id="{{APIMUserAssignedManagedIdentityId}}" output-token-variable-name="msi-access-token" ignore-error="false" />

The rest happens under the hood.

Learn more about policy creation including Microsoft Copilot usage from this article and this video. In case you fancy GitHub Copilot apply the VS Code extension. See this community post for further inspiration.

Consider the governance and security trade-offs between mighty policies and SAP OAuth clients authorized for everything vs. a multitude of clients for each API and scope.

 

Configure the policy using named values

The configuration exercise is 99% the same as outlined in the earlier blog “SAP OData connector now supports OAuth2 and SAP Principal Propagation” before. However, now we replace the client secret setting for the Entra ID app registration that represents your API Management instance with the client id of your user-assigned managed identity.

 

Fill the values as per your environment:

Key

Value sample

Hints

AADTenantId

12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz

Find it on the Azure portal (e.g. here)

APIMAADRegisteredAppClientId

999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The Application (client) id of the Entra ID app registration representing your Azure API Management instance.

APIMUserAssignedManagedIdentityId

7777abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

Client ID of user-assigned managed identity of APIM instance

AADSAPResource

https://a4h100

The provider’s name from your NetWeaver SAML setup. Typically, a URL with SID followed Client number

sap-oauth-client-username

ODATAOAUTH

User name provided on SOAUTH2 transaction (/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/oauth2_config?sap-client=100)

 

Create a named user on SU01 with minimum rights (S_SCOPE, S_SERVICE) and reference that on SOAUTH2.

Don’t forget to assign authorized scope.

sap-oauth-client-password

Password for that oauth user

This is only used to request tokens not to authenticate to SAP.

sap-oauth-scope

ZPRODUCTSVIEW_CDS_0001

The scope assigned on SOAUTH2

SAPOAuthRefreshExpiry

86400

Option to set refresh token expiry (default on SAP 2 years). It is not part of the token response.

SAPOAuthServerAdressForTokenEndpoint

a4h-internal.cloudapp.net:44301

Host and port of the target SAP OAuth server. When SAP cloud connector is used, put the virtual hostname and port.

Hints on troubleshooting

There was good feedback on the Postman collection to verify proper SAP Principal Propagation setup. Be aware that the on-behalf-of flow step to request the SAML assertion from Entra involving the managed identity cannot be tested via a REST client from your local device, because only Azure services are trusted for the request.

In general, I would recommend testing the auth flow with the least components involved as possible to reduce problem surface. Meaning, test with a REST client of your choice with client secrets to make sure that your SAP SSO setup and OAuth settings are working. Keep the API Management part and managed identity to after successful initial integration with SAP.

 

Thoughts on production readiness

Managed Identities are a well-established platform feature on Azure with large scale adoption across services. Furthermore, it is the recommended way of configuring service to service authentication.

Credentials remain in place for the SAP OAuth server as per its OAuth2 flow capabilities. Halfway there so to say. Luckily, token users have a much lower risk profile than actual users.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap 🌯. Today you saw how SAP Principal Propagation goes secret-less on Microsoft Entra ID — no passwords, and no certificates. Entra ID with Managed Identity handles it all, mapping Microsoft identities to SAP users while keeping your authorizations intact.

This is what native, secure integration should look like.

👉 If you’re still managing credentials manually in 2025, it’s time for an upgrade.

Cheers Martin
